title: Podman does not create working directory from image
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Podman does not create working directory from image `registry.svc.ci.openshift.org/openshift/release:golang-1.15`.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.  Podman run the image `registry.svc.ci.openshift.org/openshift/release:golang-1.15`

```
$ podman run -it --rm registry.svc.ci.openshift.org/openshift/release:golang-1.15 /bin/bash
Error: chdir: No such file or directory: OCI not found
```
**Describe the results you received:**

The container is NOT run.

**Describe the results you expected:**

We expect to have bash executed inside the container.


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

fc33 

```
[~]$ podman --version
podman version 2.2.1

```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


comments.body:
Image has a custom workdir - `"WorkingDir": "/go/src/github.com/openshift/origin",`

It does not exist in the image - `podman mount` shows that only the `/go` directory exists and none of the subdirectories do starting with `src/`.

Vague suspicion: I know we patched Podman to make the working directory in cases where the image specifies one that doesn't exist - maybe we're doing a Mkdir not MkdirAll so we aren't tolerant of the parent missing?
